title: PROP-FEED-S5-003 regex does not cover negative now_ms or year-10000+ cases that the i64 signature permits
description: PROP-FEED-S5-003 asserts the output matches `^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}-\d{6}-\d{3}(-\d+)?$`. The pure helper signature is `next_available_note_id(now_ms: i64, existing: &HashSet<String>)` (line 694). i64 includes negative values (pre-1970 dates) and values past year 9999. TS reference `formatBaseId(epochMillis: number)` (initialize-capture.ts:112-126) uses `new Date(epochMillis).getUTCFullYear()` which returns: a negative integer for dates before year 0 AD, and a 5-digit number for year 10000+. `pad2(-1)` returns `"-1"` (which contains a non-digit `-` and is 2 chars), so the resulting NoteId for a negative-year input contains additional `-` characters and the proptest regex would FAIL even though the function technically produced the canonical TS output. As a property test, this means: (a) if the implementer uses `proptest::prelude::any::<i64>()` as the strategy, the test will fail for legitimate-per-port outputs, leading the implementer to add `prop_assume!(now_ms >= 0)` and ship code that is semantically wrong for negative inputs (no validation at the function boundary); (b) the spec does not specify whether `now_ms < 0` is a valid input (no precondition) or whether the function must reject/saturate. Either the spec must constrain inputs (e.g., 'now_ms ∈ [0, i64::MAX]') or the regex must allow optional leading `-` and 5-digit years.
evidence:
verification-architecture.md:708 — regex `^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}-\d{6}-\d{3}(-\d+)?$`
verification-architecture.md:694 — signature uses `now_ms: i64` (no range constraint)
promptnotes/src/lib/domain/app-startup/initialize-capture.ts:114 — `const pad2 = (n: number): string => String(n).padStart(2, "0");` — does not handle negative inputs (returns '-1' unchanged because length is already 2)
promptnotes/src/lib/domain/app-startup/initialize-capture.ts:117 — `const Y = d.getUTCFullYear();` — can be negative or 5-digit
remediation: Either (a) tighten the signature in verification-architecture.md:694 to `now_ms: u64` (or wrap in a smart constructor that returns `Result<NoteId, _>` for invalid inputs and add a PROP that proves the invariant), or (b) widen the regex to `^-?\d{1,5}-\d{2}-\d{2}-\d{6}-\d{3}(-\d+)?$`. Also add an explicit AC stating which range of `now_ms` is in scope. Without this, PROP-FEED-S5-003 either crashes proptest on legitimate i64 inputs or silently restricts the test domain in a way the spec does not authorize.
